differential_diagnoses: Severe insulin resistance syndrome (Type B insulin resistance)
next_steps: Quantify insulin levels to assess for endogenous hyperinsulinemia versus exogenous administration
Confirm insulin non-adherence through supervised administration or C-peptide/insulin ratio monitoring
Perform genetic testing for lipodystrophy-related mutations
Evaluate for underlying autoimmune or hematologic processes
Consult endocrinology, rheumatology, and psychiatry for multidisciplinary management
Initiate metreleptin therapy if AGL diagnosis is confirmed

differential_diagnoses: Underlying lymphoproliferative disorder with paraneoplastic sequelae
Autoimmune polyendocrine syndrome with severe insulin resistance
Rare genetic lipodystrophy manifesting in late adulthood
next_steps: Test for anti-insulin receptor antibodies to rule out Type B insulin resistance
Perform supervised insulin administration challenge to definitively rule out factitious insulin non-adherence
Quantify serum insulin and C-peptide levels for insulin-to-C-peptide ratio assessment
Review bone marrow biopsy pathology and perform additional hematologic studies to rule out occult lymphoma or clonal processes
Measure IGF-1 levels to investigate acromegalic features in the context of hyperinsulinemia
Perform whole-body PET-CT to evaluate the splenic lesion and search for occult malignancy
Initiate multidisciplinary consultation including endocrinology, rheumatology, hematology-oncology, and psychiatry
